picks.rationale: Micron's record FQ3 revenue of $41.5B (+346% YoY), $25.11 EPS beat, and $100B in contracted AI memory revenue with $22B in customer deposits justify analyst strong_buy. Its P/E of 25.58 is the cheapest among memory peers despite +819% 1Y return. [fortune.com](https://fortune.com/2026/06/25/why-did-stock-market-tech-selloff-stop-micron-technology/)

Marvell has surged +34.3% over the past month and boasts a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.45) with a positive news sentiment of 0.368, benefiting from AI connectivity demand as part of the new 'MANIA' trade replacing the Magnificent 7.

MaxLinear is up +421.88% YTD and +580% 1Y, with 1W momentum of +8.83% and positive news sentiment 0.316. Its +3.32% 1D gain stands out against sector weakness, indicating resilient AI infrastructure demand.

Dell is up +214.93% YTD with strong 1M momentum of +30.84%, a P/E of 31.86, and the highest news sentiment (0.499) among the requested tickers. As a leading AI server provider, it is well-positioned in the AI compute buildout.

SanDisk rides Micron's coattails with a +659.6% YTD, 1M gain of +31.5%, and a buy consensus. It reported Q3 revenue of $5.95B (+251% YoY) and guided Q4 higher, supported by multi-year customer agreements validated by Micron's $100B disclosure. [247wallst.com](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/25/micron-soars-17-sandisk-jumps-15-western-digital-climbs-13-after-blowout-quarter-locks-in-100b-of-ai-memory-demand/)

Japan-listed memory stock 285A has surged +674% YTD and +33.4% in the past month. With news sentiment of 0.233 and a buy rating, it benefits from the same AI memory supply tightness, though its P/E of 87.43 is elevated.

Credo Technology has a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.32), +66.18% YTD, and +7.58% 1M momentum. With news sentiment of 0.183 and exposure to SpaceX neocloud demand, it is a key beneficiary of AI connectivity growth despite recent 1D pullback.
Hut 8 is up +139.95% YTD, +561% 1Y, and +4.57% 1M with a strong_buy consensus (score 1.29). Despite high beta of 6.04, its 1D gain of +3.88% signals resilience in AI compute demand.

Western Digital is up +212.67% YTD with a P/E of 35.1 and a dividend yield of 10%. It climbed 13% after Micron's earnings, and CEO Irving Tan confirmed strong HDD demand from AI workloads. [247wallst.com](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/25/micron-soars-17-sandisk-jumps-15-western-digital-climbs-13-after-blowout-quarter-locks-in-100b-of-ai-memory-demand/)

Talen Energy has +1.86% YTD, +6.4% 1M momentum, and a positive news sentiment of 0.319. With a buy rating and a beta of 1.6, it offers nuclear-powered AI infrastructure exposure at a more reasonable valuation than peers.

previous_picks.rationale: Blowout earnings (+346% YoY revenue to $41.5B, EPS $25.11 vs $20.49 est) and record Q4 guidance of ~$50B vs $43.2B consensus, with $100B in contracted customer agreements locking in high margins through 2030; P/E 27.4, strong_buy consensus, +284.91% YTD.

Micron's earnings reinforce NAND tightness; SNDK itself delivered record Q3 revenue of $5.95B (+97% QoQ) and guided Q4 revenue $7.75-8.25B with 79-81% gross margins; stock +748.35% YTD, +46.90% 1M, buy consensus with strong tailwinds.

Key AI interconnect play; joined S&P 500, received Nvidia strategic investment, and Jensen Huang called it the 'next trillion-dollar company'; +215% YTD, strong_buy consensus (an_score 1.45), positive news sentiment 0.21.

Memory AI play with +707.49% YTD and extremely strong 1M momentum (+46.73%); news sentiment is the most positive in the memory group (0.347) citing memory shortages beyond 2027; P/E 91, buy consensus.

AI compute leader with +222.78% YTD, +34.21% 1M momentum, strong news sentiment 0.351, and P/E 32.6 is reasonable vs growth; analyst buy consensus, benefits from AI server demand and memory supply tailwinds from Micron.

Data center connectivity play with strong_buy consensus (an_score 1.32), +87.15% YTD, +20.93% 1M, and +7.50% 1W; benefits from AI cluster scaling and data movement demand; P/E 106 reflects growth premium.

Memory/HDD play with +257.47% YTD, strong news sentiment 0.342, and P/E 97 is elevated but supported by Micron's structural demand view; analysts maintain buy ratings with price target boosts despite recent 1W pullback.

Memory and storage play up +260.09% YTD, P/E 40.5 is attractive relative to memory peers, high dividend yield 9%; buy consensus, but caution on 1W -5.16% and 1D -6.70% pullback; positive news sentiment 0.034.
Nuclear ops play with +19.64% YTD, +13.54% 1M, P/E 20.8 is the cheapest in the nuclear group; dividend yield 66% is attractive; buy consensus, benefits from AI data center power demand.
Nuclear ops play up +5.06% YTD, +7.15% 1M, +1.71% 1W with buy consensus (an_score 1.59); benefits from increasing AI-driven power demand; no P/E but momentum is improving.
